summary: Phase 4A: Cadence Governor (Frequency Caps + Time Windows). Option B chosen — READY remains channel-agnostic; fn_cadence_check is standalone evaluator called at send-attempt time. Creates lcs.cadence_policy_registry (REGISTRY, CTB-registered, composite PK lifecycle_stage+channel, CHECK constraints on channel/lifecycle/timezone_mode, updated_at trigger). Creates lcs.dispatch_attempt_log (SUPPORTING, CTB-registered, append-only — no UPDATE, no DELETE, FK to mid_ledger). Creates fn_cadence_check(uuid, text) → jsonb returning {allowed, block_reason, policy, metrics}. Evaluates min_gap_hours, max_sends_7d, max_sends_30d, allowed_days (DOW), allowed_start_local/allowed_end_local time windows. Logs ALLOWED/BLOCKED to dispatch_attempt_log, BLOCKED also to lcs_errors. Seeds 3 default EMAIL policies (QUALIFIED 72h/2x7d/5x30d, ENGAGED 48h/3x7d/8x30d, CONVERTED 24h/5x7d/12x30d). Timezone resolution stubs to UTC (ACCOUNT/COMPANY modes deferred to 4B). lcs_worker EXECUTE-only. No RAISE-after-DML. 17 enforcement tests across 5 categories.

payload.integration_decision: Option B — READY remains channel-agnostic. fn_cadence_check is standalone evaluator. Wired into fn_finalize_dispatch_batch in Phase 4B.
